as_of.title: It Takes Two to Make an Economy Go Right

inflation.label: broadening / underlying
inflation.note: Beyond gasoline she sees broad-based core pressures, with non-housing services stuck elevated and a growing risk inflation gets embedded in expectations.
inflation.quote: Looking within the components of core inflation, I see relatively broad-based price pressures across goods and nonhousing services.

labor.label: stable / balanced
labor.note: Incoming data point to a resilient, stable labor market with unemployment at 4.3 percent, around her definition of full employment.
labor.quote: In the labor market, the incoming data point to resilience and stability. The unemployment rate was 4.3 percent in April

stance.label: neutral
stance.note: Reads supportive financial conditions and a falling real funds rate as signs policy may not be restrictive, and worries it may not be sufficient to return inflation to 2 percent.
stance.quote: this is telling me that policy may not be restrictive. And rising inflation means the real federal funds rate has actually declined
